title: Synthetic conformance test-bank (CDE + crosswalk + governance)
context: The adoption milestone (adopt-001) requires that conformance be demonstrated on a named adopter's synthetic test data. A comprehensive synthetic conformance test-bank provides the reference set of test inputs and expected outputs covering the CDE schema, crosswalk round-trips, and governance checklist — giving adopters a clear target to validate against. Every fixture must be provably synthetic with attestation. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-govcheck-001 and rare-cancer-registry-templates-ig-001.
objective: Build a comprehensive synthetic conformance test-bank (`testbank/`) extending the fixtures from fixtures-001 to cover: (1) CDE schema validation cases (valid core records, valid optional modules, intentional schema violations); (2) crosswalk round-trip cases (CDE → mCODE → validate, CDE → OMOP → validate, CDE → Phenopackets → validate); and (3) governance checklist cases (compliant consent artifacts, intentional governance violations). All records are fully synthetic with attestation; tests are automated in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Test-bank includes: valid CDE core records, valid optional-module records, and intentional schema-violation cases with expected failure output.
Test-bank includes crosswalk round-trip cases for all three targets (mCODE FHIR, OMOP CDM, Phenopackets) with expected validation results.
Test-bank includes governance-checklist cases: compliant consent artifacts and cases with intentional missing fields (revocation, DUO, lawful basis, provenance) with expected failure output.
All synthetic records carry valid synthetic-data attestations; CI 'no real data' scan passes.
Automated test suite runs the test-bank against the validator and CI linters; all expected pass/fail outcomes confirmed.
`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green; maintainer sign-off recorded.
output: A `testbank/` directory with synthetic CDE, crosswalk, and governance conformance test cases and automated tests, committed via PR with green CI.
